tasks.description: Add PrimitiveOccurrencePosition (7-variant enum: NamedField/VariantField/Param/Return/Bound/TypeAliasTarget/ResultErr; Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/PartialOrd/Ord/Hash derives -- PartialOrd/Ord are newly required because PrimitiveOccurrenceReport keys a BTreeMap by this type), PrimitiveOccurrenceReport (position-keyed report: new(occurrences: BTreeMap<PrimitiveOccurrencePosition, BTreeSet<PrimitiveName>>) constructor and by_position() -> &BTreeMap<PrimitiveOccurrencePosition, BTreeSet<PrimitiveName>> accessor, replacing a prior two-bucket ordinary()/result_err() BTreeSet<PrimitiveName> accessor pair), PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError (ParseFailure { type_ref: TypeRef } for an unparseable TypeRef string, and InvalidSitePosition { position: PrimitiveOccurrencePosition } for a caller passing ResultErr as scan's position argument), and PrimitiveName (validated bare-identifier newtype: new()/as_str()) in a new domain module tddd::primitive_occurrence_scanner. Add the PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner port trait (SecondaryPort role) in the same module: scan(&self, type_ref: TypeRef, primitives: NonEmptyVec<PrimitiveName>, position: PrimitiveOccurrencePosition) -> Result<PrimitiveOccurrenceReport, PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError> -- position is the caller-known catalogue-structural site the type_ref was extracted from (NamedField/VariantField/Param/Return/Bound/TypeAliasTarget); ResultErr is scan-intrinsic-only and is never valid as this argument. Purely additive; no existing code references these types yet, so this is the first, dependency-free task. The domain crate must gain no syn dependency (CN-02) -- verified structurally via cargo make check-layers / cargo make deny, no new tooling needed. Cover with unit tests for PrimitiveName::new validation (rejects empty and non-identifier strings), PrimitiveOccurrenceReport construction and its by_position() accessor, and PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError's Display/Error impl for both variants.

Introduce RolePayloadField (new closed 8-variant enum: Invariants/Identity/ExclusiveMembers/SharedValueObjects/Emits/Handles/ReactsTo/Aggregate; Display+FromStr via strum) in domain tddd::catalogue_linter, and migrate CatalogueLinterRuleKind's 7 target_field: String fields (FieldEmpty, FieldNonEmpty, ReferencedRoleConstraint, MethodReferenceSignature, AccessorSignatureRequired, FieldElementUniqueAcrossEntries, NoExternalReferenceInMethods) to target_field: RolePayloadField. Update CatalogueLinterRule::new()'s now-unreachable target_field.is_empty() checks for these 7 variants, remove the now-unreachable CatalogueLinterRuleError::EmptyTargetField variant, introduce FreeText as the semantic wrapper for ad hoc InvalidRuleConfig diagnostic payloads, and retype CatalogueLinterRuleError::InvalidRuleConfig plus CatalogueLinterError::InvalidRuleConfig from String to FreeText, updating Display/Error/PartialEq/Eq implications and tests. Update catalogue_linter_helpers::validate_data_role_field / validate_contract_role_field (and any other target_field string-matching in catalogue_linter_eval.rs, e.g. the AccessorSignatureRequired 'identity' check) to match on RolePayloadField variants instead of raw &str, removing the now-unreachable 'not a recognised field name' catch-all arms. In the SAME commit, update usecase's lint_rule_spec_to_domain (libs/usecase/src/catalogue_lint_workflow.rs) conversion arms for these 7 LintRuleKind variants to parse the Dto's String target_field into RolePayloadField (surfacing a conversion error for unrecognised names), fix the placeholder "f" test string in test_lint_rule_spec_to_domain_converts_all_12_kinds to a real field name, and update any other usecase tests referencing target_field as a bare string. This domain+usecase bundling is required because usecase currently assigns target_field directly without parsing (verified against source): splitting the domain field-type change from its usecase consumer would not compile in this Cargo workspace. If the combined diff risks exceeding roughly 500 lines, split into two commits along variant-subset lines (e.g. FieldEmpty/FieldNonEmpty/MethodReferenceSignature/AccessorSignatureRequired first, ReferencedRoleConstraint/FieldElementUniqueAcrossEntries/NoExternalReferenceInMethods second), keeping each domain field-type change paired with its own usecase conversion update in the same commit.

Migrate CatalogueLinterRuleKind::ForbiddenMethodReceiver.forbidden_receiver from String to the pre-existing SelfReceiver enum (domain tddd::catalogue_v2::roles; Owned/SharedRef/ExclusiveRef via strum Display/EnumString), and TraitImplRequired.required_traits from NonEmptyVec<String> to NonEmptyVec<TypeRef> (pre-existing newtype, domain tddd::catalogue_v2::identifiers). Remove CatalogueLinterRule::new()'s now-redundant forbidden_receiver.is_empty() / forbidden_receiver.parse::<SelfReceiver>().is_err() validation for ForbiddenMethodReceiver (TraitImplRequired's validation arm is already a no-op and needs no change). In the SAME commit, update usecase's lint_rule_spec_to_domain conversion for these 2 variants: forbidden_receiver via forbidden_receiver.parse::<SelfReceiver>() (surfacing a conversion error on parse failure), required_traits via TypeRef::new() per element followed by NonEmptyVec::try_new(). Update any usecase tests exercising these two variants' conversions accordingly. Same cross-crate compile-safety rationale as the RolePayloadField migration: usecase currently assigns/collects these fields without validation-typed parsing.

Implement SynPrimitiveOccurrenceScanner (infrastructure tddd::syn_primitive_occurrence_scanner; SecondaryAdapter role; unit struct; Debug/Default/PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner trait impls) implementing domain's PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner port (from T001): scan(&self, type_ref: TypeRef, primitives: NonEmptyVec<PrimitiveName>, position: PrimitiveOccurrencePosition) -> Result<PrimitiveOccurrenceReport, PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError>. If position is PrimitiveOccurrencePosition::ResultErr, return PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError::InvalidSitePosition { position } immediately without scanning (ResultErr is scan-intrinsic-only and never a valid caller-supplied site). Otherwise reuse the existing type_ref_parser (libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/type_ref_parser/) to parse the TypeRef's string into a syn type (CN-01: no new parser), then perform a full syn::visit::Visit recursive walk with no per-position special-casing (OS-09), finding every ident matching a name in the requested PrimitiveName set at every position: named struct field, enum variant field, method/function param, return, bound, type alias target, and nested inside transparent containers (Option/Vec/Box/Arc/BTreeMap) and function-type signatures (Fn/FnMut/FnOnce). Ident matching is exact (IN-04: no substring matches, e.g. NonEmptyString/OsString must not false-positive on String). Label each found occurrence: occurrences found by recursing into a nested callable-type signature's own param/return slots, or a nested generic-bound clause, are labelled Param/Return/Bound respectively regardless of the caller-supplied position; occurrences found as the second (Err) type argument of a 2-generic-argument Result path segment are labelled ResultErr regardless of nesting depth (e.g. Vec<Result<T, String>>) or caller-supplied position; every other occurrence is labelled with the caller-supplied position argument (NamedField/VariantField/Param/Return/Bound/TypeAliasTarget). Collect the labelled occurrences into a PrimitiveOccurrenceReport via PrimitiveOccurrenceReport::new (a BTreeMap<PrimitiveOccurrencePosition, BTreeSet<PrimitiveName>>). Return PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError::ParseFailure { type_ref } when the TypeRef string cannot be parsed by syn. Cover with unit tests for: each caller-suppliable position kind (including TypeAliasTarget), each transparent container, function-type signatures (nested Param/Return relabeling), Result Ok-vs-Err slot distinction including nested cases (confirming ResultErr relabeling is independent of the caller-supplied position), exact-ident-match false-positive avoidance, parse failure, and InvalidSitePosition when position is passed as ResultErr.

Add a 13th CatalogueLinterRuleKind variant, ForbidPrimitiveInTypes { primitives: NonEmptyVec<PrimitiveName>, layers: NonEmptyVec<LayerId>, positions: NonEmptyVec<PrimitiveOccurrencePosition> } (domain tddd::catalogue_linter; role-axis filtering deliberately omitted from the payload, reusing RuleTarget.target_roles per CN-03), and a discriminant_name() arm for it. Add CatalogueLinterError::ScanFailed(#[from] PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError) (#[error(transparent)]). Extend evaluate_catalogue_lint (domain tddd::catalogue_linter::eval) with a new scanner: &S parameter and generic S: PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner, implementing the ForbidPrimitiveInTypes evaluation arm: for each catalogue entry selected by the rule's RuleTarget within rule.layers, iterate its TypeRef-bearing catalogue-structural slots -- named struct field (NamedField), enum variant field (VariantField), method/function param (Param), method/function return (Return), generic bound (Bound), and type_alias target (TypeAliasTarget) -- calling scanner.scan(type_ref, rule.primitives, position) once per slot with position set to that slot's own PrimitiveOccurrencePosition variant (never ResultErr, which scan reserves exclusively for its own nested-Result-error-slot detection); for each position in rule.positions, check whether the returned PrimitiveOccurrenceReport's by_position() map holds a non-empty entry for that position and emit a CatalogueLintViolation per matching (entry, position, primitive) combination; validate each of the rule's layers elements exists in all_catalogues the same way target_layer_id already does (reusing the existing UnknownLayer mechanism -- AC-11/CN-04, no 'all' sentinel). In the SAME commit, update usecase's RunCatalogueLintInteractor (libs/usecase/src/catalogue_lint_workflow.rs) to add a third generic S: PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner (+ Send + Sync in the trait impl bound), a scanner: S field, change new() from 2 to 3 parameters (loader, config_loader, scanner), and thread scanner through execute()'s call to evaluate_catalogue_lint; update the interactor's 8 existing test call sites to pass a test scanner double, and add new tests exercising the ForbidPrimitiveInTypes evaluation path end-to-end through the interactor, including a type_alias catalogue entry exercising the TypeAliasTarget slot. In the SAME commit, fix cli_composition::TrackCompositionRoot::track_lint (apps/cli-composition/src/track/tddd.rs) -- its sole RunCatalogueLintInteractor::new(loader, config_loader) call site -- to construct a SynPrimitiveOccurrenceScanner (from T004) and pass it as the third argument. This domain+usecase+cli_composition bundling is required because evaluate_catalogue_lint's signature change breaks RunCatalogueLintInteractor::execute's only call site, and RunCatalogueLintInteractor::new's arity change breaks track_lint's only construction call site -- verified against source; these three layers must land as one atomic, compiling commit. Does NOT include LintRuleKind's own 13th Dto variant or its parsing logic (deferred to T006) -- track_lint's existing behavior for the 12 pre-existing rule kinds is otherwise unchanged.

Add a 13th LintRuleKind variant, ForbidPrimitiveInTypes { primitives: Vec<String>, layers: Vec<String>, positions: Vec<String> } (usecase catalogue_lint_workflow; Dto role, raw String/Vec<String> fields matching the Dto's external-input-pending-conversion nature), and extend lint_rule_spec_to_domain to convert it into CatalogueLinterRuleKind::ForbidPrimitiveInTypes (from T005): parse each primitives element via PrimitiveName::new, each layers element via LayerId::try_new, each positions element into PrimitiveOccurrencePosition (matching the position vocabulary's canonical names), collecting each into a NonEmptyVec via NonEmptyVec::try_new, surfacing a conversion error for any empty list or any element that fails its respective parse. Add tests covering: successful conversion of a representative ForbidPrimitiveInTypes Dto value, and rejection of empty primitives/layers/positions lists and of unparseable elements in each list.

Append the 2 default ForbidPrimitiveInTypes rule declarations required by ADR D7 to BOTH .harness/catalogue-lint/config.json and .harness/catalogue-lint/presets/ddd-strict.json (the two files are kept byte-identical in this repository; update both in the same commit): (a) all roles, kind: ForbidPrimitiveInTypes { primitives: ["String"], layers: <all architecture-rules.json layer names>, positions: ["result_err"] } -- ON for every role with no exclusion (AC-05a/IN-08/CN-08); (b) the same primitives/layers, positions: ["named_field", "variant_field"], applied to all roles except Dto and Command (usecase-only) -- ErrorType and every other non-Dto/Command role remain included (AC-05b/IN-09/CN-09). Do not enable non-String primitives or general param/return positions by default (AC-07/IN-10/OS-03). This task must land after T006, since FsLintConfigLoader parses the whole config file upfront and an unparseable rule kind would break the existing sotp track lint command for every layer, not just the new rule. Add an integration-level test (or extend the existing apps/cli/tests/cli_track_lint.rs fixture suite) verifying the default config parses successfully and that a fixture catalogue entry with a bare String named field triggers a violation while the same entry under a Dto role does not, and that a Result<T, String> field's Err slot still violates regardless of role.

Add a new bin/sotp subcommand (or an equivalent composition-root method) that runs the default-config catalogue lint across every layer of the active track and exits non-zero if any layer reports a violation, fully reusing the existing active-track-resolution mechanism already used by track lint / signal calc-impl-catalog (no new track-scoping logic -- CN-07). Add a Makefile.toml task wrapping this subcommand with no embedded shell logic (a thin command+args wrapper, consistent with existing patterns), and insert it into the track-active-gate dependency chain (or an equivalent always-run gate reached by track-commit-message and track-local-review) so the lint is enforced as a blocking gate from the moment this track lands, with no warn-only intermediate stage (AC-10/IN-12/OS-06). Add a minimal CLI integration test verifying non-zero exit when a fixture catalogue has a violation and zero exit when it does not. Expected to be small and standalone: on the order of a Makefile.toml edit, one or two new/modified CLI-layer Rust files, and one test file -- do not fuse with any of the domain/usecase/infrastructure tasks.

summary: Adds a catalogue-lint rule kind (ForbidPrimitiveInTypes) that mechanically detects and blocks configured primitive types (default: String) appearing in type-catalogue named/variant fields and Result Err slots, as a meta-lint enforcing the newtype design judgement prefer-type-safe-abstractions.md's Decision Flow cannot itself enforce (GO-01).

Detection reuses the existing syn-based TypeRef parser via a full syn::visit::Visit recursive walk with no position special-casing; the engine only reports where a primitive occurs, and config alone decides which positions are enforced (GO-02).

The occurrence-detection judgement is defined as a domain port (PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner) implemented by a syn-based infrastructure adapter, preserving domain's syn-independence; the new rule variant reuses the existing RuleTarget role-axis mechanism rather than adding a role field (GO-03).

Applicability is configured declaratively across a (layer x role x position x primitive) 4-axis grid on the existing .harness/catalogue-lint/ config surface, with layer names sourced from architecture-rules.json (no 'all' sentinel), defaulting to excluding only Dto (all layers) and Command (usecase) from field-position checks while keeping result_err enforcement universal, wired in as a blocking gate from the start (GO-04).

This track also performs a self-referential (dogfooding) fix: CatalogueLinterRuleKind's own pre-existing String-typed fields (target_field, forbidden_receiver, required_traits) are migrated to validated domain types (RolePayloadField, SelfReceiver, TypeRef) so the new rule, once active, does not immediately flag the rule-kind type that implements it.

Task sequencing follows the SoT chain order (domain port/types -> domain self-application refactor -> infrastructure adapter -> domain+usecase+cli_composition wiring -> usecase Dto exposure -> default config -> gate wiring), with cross-crate atomic bundling wherever a Cargo workspace compile-order dependency would otherwise be broken across a task boundary (documented per-task above).

sections.title: Domain foundation: primitive-occurrence port and value types
description: Introduces the PrimitiveOccurrenceScanner port and its supporting value/error types (PrimitiveOccurrencePosition, PrimitiveOccurrenceReport, PrimitiveOccurrenceScanError, PrimitiveName) in the domain layer. Purely additive; no existing code references these types yet, so this lands safely as the first task.

sections.title: Self-application refactor: CatalogueLinterRuleKind dogfooding fix
description: Migrates CatalogueLinterRuleKind's pre-existing String-typed fields to validated domain types, resolving the tension where this track's own primitive-obsession rule would otherwise flag the very type implementing it. Each domain field-type change is bundled with its required usecase-layer conversion-function update in the same commit, since usecase currently assigns these fields without parsing and a Cargo workspace compiles all crates together.

sections.title: Detection engine and cross-crate wiring
description: Implements the syn-based occurrence-scanning infrastructure adapter, then threads it through the domain evaluation function and usecase interactor as a new 13th CatalogueLinterRuleKind variant. The domain signature change, usecase interactor arity change, and cli_composition's sole construction call site are bundled into one atomic commit because each breaks the next's compilation otherwise.

sections.title: Rule exposure, default configuration, and gate wiring
description: Exposes the new rule kind through the usecase-layer LintRuleKind Dto and its conversion logic, declares the ADR D7 default rule configuration on the existing .harness/catalogue-lint/ config surface, and wires the lint as a blocking, active-track-scoped gate via a new bin/sotp subcommand and Makefile.toml chain entry.
